name: Thomas Wren
role: Innkeeper of the Green Dragon Tavern, Sons of Liberty coordinator
publicFace: A prosperous, cheerful tavern keeper who is good at remembering faces and forgetting conversations.
privateGoal: To receive the dispatch and route map, verify them, and dispatch riders to Lexington and Concord before midnight.
fear: That the network has been compromised and tonight's delivery is a trap.
knowledge: He is expecting a delivery tonight but not necessarily from the player — he will need the recognition phrase.
He knows the riders are ready and waiting in the stable behind the tavern.
He has heard that a checkpoint has been set up on Ann Street and has already sent one person back.
voice: Warm, loud in the public room, very quiet in the back — two entirely different men separated by a door.
trustLogic: He opens up immediately to the recognition phrase. Without it, he is a pleasant wall. He will also respond to Hannah Cross's name, spoken quietly, as a secondary credential.
secrets: He has a second copy of the route map hidden in the tavern — he will not reveal this unless the first is lost.
He knows who the second courier is and will activate them if the primary delivery fails.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: He smiles and offers a drink and talks about nothing.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: He calls for his tapman by name — a signal — and the back door of the tavern opens.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will never confirm that riders are waiting. That is the piece that cannot be known.
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: If soldiers enter the tavern in force.
aggressionProfile.fleeStyle: He goes through the kitchen and into the stable yard — he has done this before.
aggressionProfile.chaseStyle: He does not run. He walks into a crowd and becomes unremarkable.
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: He gives his name, his trade, and invites the officer to inspect his license. He is very calm.
